title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_37 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: At p = 37 the old fixed barrier B = {1,19,23,29} fails only as a wrapper by spill count, while a moved sparse barrier B = {1,43,47,53} still supports an explicit verified witness. This points toward a movable-barrier slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25) rather than a graph-level breakpoint at p = 37.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_37 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification. The campaign-relevant gain is that p = 37 currently looks like the first failure of one fixed wrapper, not the first failure of the graph family itself: moving the barrier clique up to B = {1,43,47,53} still closes the instance. This is feeder evidence for a movable sparse-barrier slice on Γ(Z_p × Z_25), not yet a proved family theorem.
candidate_theorem_slice: A movable sparse-barrier sufficient-condition slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): if N = 5p + 19 admits a pairwise-coprime barrier B = {1,q1,q2,q3} whose nontrivial multiples in {1,...,N} occupy at most the 20 A-slots, and if there are p - 1 primes disjoint from the factor palette used by A ∪ B for C, then assigning those barrier multiples to A, those primes to C, and the complement to D yields a prime labeling.
next_action: promote the verified p = 37 movable-barrier feeder into the family generalization dossier, then test z41-z25-prime-zero-divisor-graph next; keep z43-z25-prime-zero-divisor-graph as the next pressure point and do not run Lean on this isolated instance yet
